records.ety: [AS. refian, from ref spoil, plunder, clothing, refan to break (cf. birefan to deprive of); akin to G. rauben to rob, Icel. raufa to rob, rjūfa to break, violate, Goth. birubōn to despoil, L. rumpere to break; cf. Skr. lup to break. √114. Cf. Bereave, Rob, v. t., Robe, Rove, v. i., Rupture.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Reaved (rēvd), Reft (rft), or Raft (rft) (obs.); p. pr. & vb. n. Reaving.]

defs: To take away by violence or by stealth; to snatch away; to rob; to despoil; to bereave. [Archaic].
quotes: He golden apples raft of the dragon.
If the wooers reave By privy stratagem my life at home.
To reave the orphan of his patrimony.
The heathen caught and reft him of his tongue.
